items.title: Gold falls 1% after Fed holds rates steady, signals rate hike this year
items.summary: Gold prices fell significantly on Wednesday. The U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged but indicated a possible increase later this year. This move strengthened the U.S. dollar, making gold more expensive for international buyers. Markets now anticipate a higher chance of a rate hike in December. Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also experienced declines.

items.title: Dollar jumps as Fed holds rates but projects one hike later this year
items.summary: The dollar strengthened as the Federal Reserve held interest rates steady but signaled a potential hike later this year, with policymakers raising inflation projections. New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's revised statement removed forward guidance, impacting market expectations. Meanwhile, other central banks like the BOE and BOJ are also navigating inflation concerns.

items.title: Peace deal: Cautious Indian shipowners seek govt. help
items.summary: Thirteen Indian-flagged vessels are still in the Persian Gulf, west of the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: US stocks: Nasdaq, S&P fall over 1% as Fed holds rates; traders raise hike bets
items.summary: US stocks tumbled Wednesday as traders anticipated a Federal Reserve rate hike, with policymakers projecting rising interest rates later this year. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh emphasized taming inflation, shifting sentiment from expected rate cuts. This hawkish tilt led to significant market declines across major indices.

items.title: US Fed keeps rates unchanged as policymakers signal tighter policy ahead. What does it mean for Indian stock market?
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds rate at 3.50%-3.75%, addressing inflation concerns from rising energy prices. Officials updated year-end PCE inflation forecasts, hinting at potential rate hike that may impact foreign investment sentiment, particularly in emerging markets like India.

items.title: US-Iran Deal Update: Trump says pact likely soon, signing expected by June 19
items.summary: On Monday, both the US and Iran indicated that they had reached a final peace pact in their nearly four-month-long conflict that began with joint American-Israeli strikes on February 28 that killed Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Govt announces key leadership changes in insurance sector
items.summary: Dinesh Pant, managing director at Life Insurance Corporation of India, has been appointed whole-time member (actuary) at IRDAI, while Girija Subramanian, chairman-cum-managing director of The New India Assurance Company Limited, has been appointed whole-time member (distribution). Both appointments are for five years. The government has also reappointed Deepak Sood as whole-time member (non-life), extending his term by two years until 2028, and Rajay Kumar Sinha as whole-time member (finance and investment) for another two years until 2029, or until they attain the age of 65 years, whichever i

items.title: Financial Intelligence Unit puts large crypto OTC deals under scanner, asks exchanges for trade data
items.summary: Indian financial authorities are examining large over-the-counter cryptocurrency trades. The Financial Intelligence Unit is requesting data from major crypto exchanges for transactions exceeding $10,000. This move aims to combat money laundering and track beneficial owners. These off-market deals are insulated from price volatility. The focus is on ensuring transparency and preventing illicit activities within the digital asset space.

items.title: Bollywood's box office blues return after one big hit
items.summary: Hindi cinema is experiencing a box-office slump after a recent success. Several films with known casts have failed to attract audiences. Industry experts point to old storytelling, declining star appeal, and a disconnect with viewer tastes. Producers struggle to build pre-release buzz. The focus is now on delivering the right content at the right time to revive the industry.

items.title: NARCL makes fifth exit with Rs 575 crore recovery
items.summary: Agson Global, a manufacturer of aroma ingredients, was admitted to insolvency proceedings by the National Company Law Tribunal following a petition filed by the erstwhile Allahabad Bank, now part of Indian Bank. NARCL, the state-backed "bad bank", had acquired Agson Global's debt at a steep discount, said one of the people cited earlier. The recovery enabled NARCL to fully redeem the security receipts (SRs) issued to lenders and also distribute an additional nearly Rs 200 crore to the banks.

items.title: Consumer groups oppose Trai plan to scrap advisory panel for telecom complaints
items.summary: Consumer advocates are pushing back against the telecom regulator's plan to eliminate the advisory committee for complaint appeals. They argue this move would remove a vital safeguard for consumers. Instead, they propose modernizing the committee with digital tools and faster turnaround times. Telecom operators, however, support the regulator's proposal.

items.title: NSE IPO News Confirmed: Exchange files papers with SEBI to float OFS; SBI to be biggest seller—FULL details
items.summary: Stock exchange NSE's proposed IPO will be entirely an offer for sale (OFS), meaning the bourse will receive no proceeds from the issue. State Bank of India -- the country's largest lender by assets -- will be the biggest selling shareholder in the public offer.

items.title: US buyers keen to stitch deals with Indian apparel exporters
items.summary: Indian apparel exporters face pressure as US buyers demand discounts of up to 10%. This comes as India's readymade garment exports have declined for six consecutive months, largely due to weaker demand in the United States. Competitors like Vietnam and Indonesia are gaining ground.

items.title: Airlines may fly low this fiscal
items.summary: Indian airlines face a profit drop this financial year. Higher fuel prices, airspace limits, and a weaker rupee are increasing costs. Fleet expansion will also raise lease payments. While conflict resolution could ease fuel expenses, airlines will need to manage these pressures. This situation impacts their financial performance.

items.title: Govt waives customs overtime charges for cruise passengers
items.summary: International cruise passengers will no longer pay merchant overtime charges for customs clearance. This decision aims to boost cruise tourism and simplify passenger processing. The move follows requests from the shipping ministry and industry stakeholders. It is expected to reduce operational costs and enhance the competitiveness of Indian cruise destinations. This exemption applies to designated round-the-clock customs ports.

items.title: Kudankulam nuclear plant unit-5 gets reactor vessel
items.summary: India's Nuclear Power Corporation has achieved a significant milestone. The reactor pressure vessel for Kudankulam Unit-5 has been installed. This crucial step was completed with support from Russia's Rosatom. The installation marks progress in India's nuclear energy expansion. This project underscores strong India-Russia cooperation in nuclear technology. It is vital for India's energy security goals.

items.title: Kitchen staples face rising price pressure as El Nino risks grow
items.summary: Expect higher prices for essential kitchen items like spices, eggs, and dairy. Food inflation is rising, with a potential monsoon deficit and heatwaves impacting crop yields. Economists predict price pressures to increase in the coming months. This will affect the cost of daily groceries for households across India.

items.title: Oil below $80 may cap fuel price hikes, boost India outlook
items.summary: Falling crude oil prices below $80 a barrel are set to prevent further fuel price increases. This development is boosting India's economic outlook, with economists predicting improved inflation and growth. Lower oil costs will also help narrow the current account deficit. While wholesale inflation may react quickly, retail inflation depends on oil marketing companies' decisions.

items.title: Non-fossil power capacity set to cross 300 GW by Sept, says energy minister Pralhad Joshi
items.summary: India's non-fossil fuel power capacity will surpass 300 GW by September. This expansion is crucial for green hydrogen production under the National Green Hydrogen Mission. The mission aims for significant green hydrogen output, requiring substantial renewable energy. Private sector participation is strong. States are encouraged to adopt green hydrogen policies to attract investment and boost the sector's growth.

items.title: Govt readies farm safety net with banks, insurers as El Nino looms
items.summary: The government is enhancing financial safety nets for rural families. This initiative aims to cushion the impact of El Nino on farming. Efforts include improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be utilized. The Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana is also under review for broader coverage.

items.title: Fed holds rates steady, officials split over possible hikes this year
items.summary: The meeting, chaired for the first time by Kevin Warsh, signalled a shift in the Fed’s communication strategy as it grapples with rising inflation pressures from energy costs and geopolitical tensions

items.title: ISWAI welcomes India-UK FTA, hails lower tariffs on Scotch as boost for Indian spirits sector
items.summary: The International Spirits and Wines Association of India has welcomed the newly implemented India-UK Free Trade Agreement, calling it a landmark milestone that will significantly boost bilateral trade and strengthen India's alcoholic beverage industry

items.title: India readies Rs 12,000 crore push for local battery component manufacturing
items.summary: India is set to launch a new incentive scheme to boost local manufacturing of advanced cell battery components. This initiative aims to reduce import dependence for crucial materials like cathode and anode active materials, electrolysers, and copper foil separators. The scheme, with an estimated ₹12,000 crore allocation, will encourage domestic production and supply chain development.

items.title: India’s crude oil, LNG imports surge in May although bill continues to stay elevated
items.summary: India paid 14.7% more in May for its crude imports compared to April and more than 22% higher for LNG.

items.title: Insurers face margin pressure amid rising competition, costs; rural protection gap persists: Report
items.summary: Indian insurers are feeling the pinch from fierce price wars and increasing operational expenses. To thrive, they must manage capital wisely, focus on solid underwriting, and control costs. Technology and a shift towards protection products will be key to reaching more customers and rebuilding trust. Broader financial services also see growth opportunities in MSME lending and corporate banking.

items.title: RBI allows banks to offer higher interest rates to NRIs/PIOs to mobilise forex
items.summary: As per the directions interest rates on NRE/NRO deposits will not be higher than those offered by the bank on comparable domestic rupee term deposits

items.title: Despite crisis we see momentum in market: Mercedes-Benz India chief
items.summary: To offset rising cost pressure, company evaluating 3rd price hike this year

items.title: US Fed keeps rates unchanged for fourth straight meeting at 3.5%–3.75%: 5 key takeaways
items.summary: The Federal Reserve has held its interest rates steady as it assesses inflation and growth dynamics. The recent surge in inflation, driven by energy prices, raises expectations for a possible rate hike later this year amid ongoing economic challenges.

items.title: US Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged, projects one rate hike for 2026
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady at 3.5%-3.75% in Kevin Warsh's first meeting, with policymakers signaling potential rate hikes to combat surging inflation. Despite economic expansion and strong job gains, elevated inflation, partly due to Middle East conflict and energy prices, is a growing concern.

items.title: NSE IPO back on track as exchange files prospectus with SEBI for ₹30,000-crore issue
items.summary: Pure offer-for-sale of up to 149 million equity shares likely to be one of India’s biggest public issues of the year

items.title: India-UK FTA draws mixed response from liquor industry over Scotch duty cuts
items.summary: The India-UK Free Trade Agreement is set to begin on July 15. Alcoholic beverage makers welcome the pact, expecting increased trade and a boost for premium Indian spirits. Imported liquor brands may see reduced concessions in some states. This agreement aims to make premium international brands more accessible to Indian consumers, supporting growth in related sectors.

items.title: Exide to balance lead-acid strength with lithium-ion push
items.summary: Exide Industries is strategically expanding into lithium-ion cell manufacturing to support India's energy transition. The company will continue its strong lead-acid battery business. Exide Energy Solutions Ltd is engaging with manufacturers for electric vehicles and energy storage. India's lithium-ion battery demand is projected to grow significantly by 2030, driven by electric mobility.

items.title: Realtors eye margin relief as US-Iran peace deal raises hopes of lower fuel costs
items.summary: A potential peace deal between the US and Iran offers hope for India's real estate and construction sectors. Developers are watching oil prices closely. A ceasefire could lower crude oil costs, easing supply chain worries. This may gradually reduce construction expenses and improve project margins. Stability in the Gulf region could also boost housing demand from non-resident Indians.

items.title: Credit flow to bottom tier slows as microfinance lenders turn to safer borrowers: SIDBI-Equifax
items.summary: Credit access for bottom-of-the-pyramid borrowers in India has slowed as microfinance lenders turn cautious after two years of asset quality stress, increasingly favouring customers with established credit histories. A joint SIDBI-Equifax study shows the share of new-to-credit borrowers fell sharply to 20% from 33% three years ago, reflecting tighter risk filters.

items.title: U.S.-Iran deal could remove restrictions on tankers; unlock banking, insurance access
items.summary: Before the conflict, international shipping through the Strait of Hormuz operated without tolls, service fees or any mandatory reporting to national authorities; while the U.S. says no tolls should be imposed, Iran says ships transiting the strait will have to pay a service fee and report to authorities

items.title: Leapfrog Engineering IPO subscribed 1.28 times on day 1, QIB demand leads surge
items.summary: The Bengaluru-based EPCC company plans to use proceeds for setting up an assembling unit, working capital needs and general corporate purposes

items.title: DOMS Block Deal Details: FILA sells 42.48 lakh shares in Rs 934 crore transaction
items.summary: Promoter FILA trims stake in stationery maker through a Rs 934 crore block deal; Axis Mutual Fund and SBI Mutual Fund among key buyers.

items.title: Who is Michael Truell, the 25-year-old founder whose AI startup SpaceX acquired for $60 billion?
items.summary: Cursor said its products are used by 64% of Fortune 500 companies, and that its tools write more than 100 million lines of code a day for enterprise customers.

items.title: IPO comeback: ₹60,000 crore pipeline in next three months
items.summary: This resurgence follows a six-month slowdown, driven by easing geopolitical tensions, a rally in equity markets, and declining crude prices.

items.title: Modi, Trump talk trade, seafarer safety on G7 sidelines
items.summary: While Trump called Modi a “tough negotiator” on the interim trade deal, discussions are weighed down by recent maritime deaths and ongoing US tariff investigations

items.title: JLR projects revenue growth and return to profit in FY27, doubles down on US
items.summary: Jaguar Land Rover anticipates a strong comeback in financial year 2027. The company projects a 13% revenue increase and a return to profitability. This recovery will be powered by a strategic emphasis on North America, its key growth market. JLR aims for £26 billion in revenue and improved Ebit margins.

items.title: NSE files DRHP for potentially India’s biggest-ever IPO, valuation seen at  ₹5 trillion
items.summary: The NSE IPO is pegged at roughly  ₹29,780 crore in the grey market. At that size, the IPO valuation is seen at over  ₹5 trillion.

items.title: NSE files DRHP for mega $3 billion IPO, SBI among 10 investors to sell stake
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange of India has filed draft papers for its initial public offering. This move positions NSE for one of the country's major IPOs this year. It follows a previous attempt in 2016 that was delayed. NSE is India's largest stock exchange and a leading global derivatives market. This listing is highly anticipated.

items.title: HDFC Bank bond issue priced at 90 bps above US Treasury
items.summary: HDFC Bank raised $750 million through five-year dollar bonds at 90 basis points over US Treasuries to fund its overseas operations.&nbsp;

items.title: Multibagger defence stock NIBE to be in focus on Thursday after demonstrating Garudastra to Indian Army
items.summary: NIBE's defence stock garners attention after showcasing the Garudastra mortar system to the Indian Army, demonstrating advanced capabilities in GPS and laser-guided precision strikes. The system can rapidly engage targets and has shown impressive performance metrics during trials.

items.title: DGCA asks operators of uncontrolled airfields to review ops conditions, rectify deficiencies
items.summary: Aviation watchdog DGCA has ordered operators of uncontrolled airstrips to immediately review their facilities. This action follows recent accidents at such airfields. Operators must ensure strict compliance with safety norms and rectify any deficiencies. The DGCA also advised obtaining an aerodrome license for regularly used airstrips. This move aims to enhance safety for all aircraft operations across the country.

items.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
items.summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.

items.title: Tomato, onion, potato prices rise within a week, yet farmers say their produce fetches very low prices
items.summary: ‘In potato-growing States such as U.P., and Bihar too, farmers are in distress as they get as little as ₹2 or ₹3 per kilo for potatoes. But consumers are paying through their nose,’ says farmer leader Ashok Dhawale

items.title: E100: How India’s new flex fuel norms affect your vehicle and wallet | Business Matters
items.summary: The government has approved rules for 100% ethanol (E100) fuel. What does this mean for motorists? From higher car costs to lower mileage, here’s a simple breakdown of flex-fuel vehicles, costs, and impact on your everyday driving.

items.title: Oil prices tumble to 3-and-half month low as potential peace deal signing nears
Chennai Port Authority offers wharfage concessions up to 80% for non-containerised cargo
items.summary: Designed to reward growth and encourage new business, the scheme provides attractive incentives for both existing and new customers routing cargo through Chennai Port

items.title: Attracting NRI inflows: RBI temporarily withdraws interest rate ceiling on fresh FCNR(B) deposits of 3-5 yr tenor
items.summary: Removes the restriction on interest rates on NRE deposits of 3 year and above tenors.

items.title: Jeh Aerospace signs multi-year manufacturing pact with Solestra for dedicated unit in Hyderabad
items.summary: Hyderabad's Jeh Aerospace is partnering with North America's Solestra Group to establish a dedicated manufacturing center. This Global Manufacturing Center will serve as an exclusive production cell for Solestra, enhancing its capacity and cost-competitiveness. The collaboration aims to diversify global aerospace supply chains and build manufacturing strength outside traditional hubs, focusing on high-precision mechanical systems for aero-engine and aircraft applications.

items.title: RBI temporarily withdraws rate caps on FCNR(B), NRE deposits till Sept 30
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has removed interest rate caps on specific non-resident deposits. This move grants banks more freedom to attract foreign funds. The changes are effective immediately and will continue until September 2026. Banks can now offer higher rates on FCNR(B) and NRE deposits. This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

items.title: Tata Trusts backs IIM Bangalore’s undergraduate school with undisclosed grant
items.summary: The inaugural batch will comprise 80 students, with 40 students in each programme. The curriculum combines economics, data science and business studies

items.title: Pakistan duty drawback benefits may spoil India’s Basmati party
items.summary: Islamabad plans to extend duty drawback benefit for the fragrant rice export beyond June, when trade via Strait of Hormuz is set to normalise

items.title: India & U.K. overcome last-minute steel hurdle, announce July 15 as trade deal implementation date
items.summary: The India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement was signed in July 2025 and was set to be implemented by April or early May 2026. However, a fresh regulation on steel imports by the U.K. acted as a late-stage hiccup

items.title: Domestic tractor wholesale volumes likely to grow at 1-4% in FY27: ICRA
items.summary: Tractor sales are set for modest growth this fiscal year. However, a below-normal monsoon forecast poses a risk to agricultural output and sales. Despite this, manufacturers' margins are expected to remain healthy. Strong farm cash flows and government support continue to underpin the sector. Industry growth may moderate due to an elevated base and potential El Nino conditions.

items.title: I Squared, US DFC to set up $3 billion energy infrastructure platform in Asia
items.summary: I Squared Capital and the US International Development Finance Corporation are partnering. They will create a three billion dollar investment platform. This platform will focus on energy infrastructure in South and Southeast Asia. The goal is to boost energy connectivity with the Indo-Pacific region. It will support US LNG and energy exports.

items.title: HFCL bags Rs 2,666 crore telcom gear supply order from Rail Vikas Nigam for BharatNet project
items.summary: HFCL has secured a significant Rs 2,666 crore order from Rail Vikas Nigam Limited. This contract is for the BharatNet Phase-3 project in Uttar Pradesh West. HFCL will supply telecom equipment and manage installation and commissioning. The project includes network creation and maintenance over a considerable period. This order adds to previous contracts for the same project.

items.title: India-UK free trade pact to take effect July 15 after breakthrough in steel deadlock
items.summary: Indian exporters of textiles, footwear, gems & jewellery to gain; social security pact to protect short-term expat workers from being double taxed

items.title: RBI conducts two VRR auctions to address liquidity tightness in banking system
items.summary: The VRR auctions infused liquidity amounting to ₹72,300 crore in the banking system at a weighted average rate of 5.26%

items.title: Grooming and oral care segments have driven broad-based growth at Gillette India over past 5 years
items.summary: We continue to strengthen our market leadership in segments like Blades and Razors, and are driving category growth in segments like Female Grooming: Srividya Srinivasan, Chief Financial Officer, Gillette India,

items.title: Bhartiya Urban will invest Rs 1,000 crore in developing 1.5 million sq ft residential development in North Bengaluru
items.summary: Bhartiya Urban is launching Nikoo Homes 8 in Bengaluru's Thanisandra corridor. This project spans 11 acres and includes over 1,000 homes. It offers a range of options from studios to luxury villas. The development has a business potential of Rs 2000 crore. Bhartiya Urban plans further launches and inventory additions across multiple locations.

items.title: Microfinance sector sees 17% contraction in  portfolio in FY26: SIDBI-Equifax report
items.summary: The POS of the microfinance sector declined to ₹2,77,053 crore as at March-end 2026 from ₹3,35,060 crore as at March-end 2025 and ₹3,77,706 crore as at March-end 2024

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian equities advanced for a fourth straight session as easing geopolitical tensions, lower crude oil prices, softer bond yields and a stronger rupee boosted sentiment. Gains in IT, metals and PSU banks lifted benchmarks, while India VIX remained subdued amid improving market confidence.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold and silver remain steady ahead of US Fed policy decision
items.summary: Precious metals showed little movement as traders awaited the US Federal Reserve's interest-rate decision. Gold futures rose slightly to $4,369, while silver gained to over $70, amid geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns following potential US-Iran peace talks.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Titagarh Rail among 2 stock recommendations for Thursday
items.summary: Indian equities extended their recovery amid easing US-Iran tensions and softer crude prices. Analysts recommended Titagarh Rail Systems and Engineers India, citing bullish technical patterns, strong momentum, improving volumes and favorable positioning above key moving averages for potential near-term gains.

items.title: Gig worker unions seek clarity on benefits as e-Shram registration deadline nears
items.summary: Worker representatives say the larger issue is not registration itself

items.title: India’s electronics output hits ₹13 lakh crore; Vaishnaw eyes No. 2 export spot
items.summary: India's electronics production has reached Rs 13 lakh crore. The nation aims to make electronics the second largest export category. Two semiconductor plants are already operational. Two more chip plants will begin full commercial production this year. This growth is driven by smartphone exports, with Apple being a major contributor.

items.title: Wall Street edges higher ahead of Federal Reserve rate decision, Broadcom jumps 4.4%, Applied Materials surges 8.6%
items.summary: At 10:15 a.m. Eastern Time, the S&amp;P 500 gained 0.2%, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average advanced 0.5%. The Nasdaq Composite also rose 0.2%

items.title: BRND.ME transitions to a public company ahead of planned IPO
items.summary: The conversion into a public company brings BRND.ME under a structure that is more aligned with public-market requirements, enabling greater governance clarity and regulatory alignment

items.title: InCred Capital expands global footprint with launch of US operations; appoints Arunava Das as CEO, North America
items.summary: InCred Capital, the institutional, wealth and asset management arm of InCred Group, announced the launch of its US operations with the opening of its New York office.

items.title: Block Deal Buzz: DOMS Industries trade volatile as promoter FILA likely sells 7.3% stake worth nearly Rs 981 crore
items.summary: Stock swings after a large transaction involving 44.1 lakh shares; Zee Business had reported that promoter FILA was looking to sell up to a 7 per cent stake through a block deal

items.title: Civil aviation min to upgrade AirSewa portal, tap AI for faster passengers' grievances redressal
items.summary: The Civil Aviation Ministry is enhancing the AirSewa portal and integrating artificial intelligence into the Passenger Assistance Control Room. This initiative aims to address air passenger grievances swiftly and effectively. The Passenger Assistance Control Room has already resolved over 73,000 complaints with a high success rate. Future upgrades will ensure a more seamless platform for passenger support.

items.title: Rupee appreciates for fourth consecutive session as crude cools
UK-India free trade deal to kick in on July 15, cutting tariffs by over $480 mn in year one
items.summary: The United Kingdom and India will implement their Free Trade Agreement on July 15. This landmark deal, signed last year, promises to boost trade significantly. Businesses have 28 days to prepare for the changes. Tariffs on many UK exports to India will be reduced, including Scotch whisky and automotive products. India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

items.title: BMW India aims to cross 20,000 unit sales mark in 2026, launches new India made MINI Countryman C
items.summary: The introductory price of MINI Countryman C is ₹47.50 lakh

items.title: G7 Summit: India trade deal 'very close', says Trump after meeting with PM Modi—Key takeaways
items.summary: A key meeting between PM Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the G7 Summit concluded on a positive note. The June 17 meeting in France marked the first face-to-face interaction between the two leaders since the PM's visit to Washington in February 2025.

items.title: Centre opens selection for chiefs of SJVN and NTPC Green Energy
items.summary: Top officials from the private sector can also apply to these two top posts

items.title: SEBI plugs long-standing gap in AIF winding-up process
items.summary: The framework allows AIFs to retain liquidation proceeds in specified cases, including pending or anticipated litigation, tax or regulatory demands, and residual winding-up expenses

items.title: Axis Bank witnesses high double-digit decline in frauds, backed by AI-led intelligence system
items.summary: The bank witnessed around 40 per cent reduction in fraud incidents in last financial year compared to previous financial year

items.title: India on track to meet 3% compressed biogas blending target for current financial year: officials
items.summary: India has a target of blending 3% CBG with CNG and domestic PNG in the ongoing financial year, 4% in the next financial year and 5% by FY 2028-29

items.title: EV industry seeks financing, payment security support to drive adoption
items.summary: Electric vehicle makers are asking the government for better financing and payment security. This is to speed up the use of electric vehicles and boost local production. Industry leaders met with Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy to discuss challenges. They also want more charging stations and faster approvals for new technologies. The minister assured support for the EV sector's growth.

items.title: HDFC MF, ADIA among buyers as Sepia Investments offloads Rs 749 crore in Corona Remedies via block deal
items.summary: Sepia Investments and Anchor Partners sold shares worth about Rs 777 crore in Corona Remedies through block deals. HDFC Mutual Fund emerged as the largest buyer, while several institutional investors, including Aberdeen entities, ADIA, Invesco and WhiteOak, also accumulated stakes.

items.title: India's hydrogen policy is ahead of many countries: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
items.summary: A Rs 20,000 crore scheme has been introduced for the 'Green Hydrogen Mission'. This policy places India well ahead of many other countries, said Joshi.

items.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to do fodder planning to avoid a potential shortage due to El Nino
items.summary: Maharashtra farmers are urged to plan for fodder shortages due to El Nino. The Animal Husbandry Department advises cultivating nutritious, multipurpose fodder crops. Farmers should also preserve natural grass and store excess fodder as silage. This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.

items.title: Embassy Office Parks REIT approves Rs 700 crore NCD issuance via private placement
items.summary: Embassy Office Parks REIT has approved the issuance of Rs 700 crore worth of non-convertible debentures (NCDs). According to the exchange filing, the company will issue 70,000 listed, rated, secured, redeemable, transferable, rupee-denominated NCDs with a face value of Rs 1,00,000 each, aggregating to Rs 700 crore.

items.title: Govt's 5% GIC Re offer for sale fully subscribed; boosts FY27 divestment drive
items.summary: Strong investor response triggered the full greenshoe option, despite a sharp 9% drop in the state-owned reinsurer's share price during the transaction.

items.title: PM Modi-Trump G7 Summit Meeting Today: Trade deal, H-1B, West Asia likely on agenda
items.summary: The final day of the G7 Summit is expected to focus on strengthening global partnerships and rebuilding international solidarity among member economies.

items.title: Turtlemint Fintech Solutions IPO to open on Friday: 10 key things to know from RHP before you subscribe to  ₹883 crore
items.summary: Turtlemint's IPO opens for subscription from June 19 to June 23, aiming to raise  ₹882.67 crore. The company reserves 75% for QIBs, 15% for NIIs, and 10% for retail investors, with shares priced between  ₹144-155. Listing is expected on June 29.

items.title: Target Corp leases 8.3 lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru for 10 years
items.summary: US retail giant Target Corporation secures a significant office space in Bengaluru. The company will occupy over 8.3 lakh square feet at Embassy Manyata Business Park. This long-term lease, valued at approximately Rs 1,250 crore, highlights the growing presence of global capability centers in India's tech hub. The deal underscores sustained demand for Grade A office spaces in Bengaluru.

items.title: Rupee hits six-week high on RBI measures and dollar inflow hopes
items.summary: The rupee extended its gains to close at 94.53 against the US dollar, supported by RBI measures to boost foreign currency inflows, softer crude prices and expectations of stronger FCNR-B deposit inflows. The currency touched a six-week high before trimming intraday gains.

items.title: Automakers step in as supply chain talent crunch bites
items.summary: Automakers are increasingly stepping in to help vendors recruit, train and retain workers, as labour shortages and specialised skill gaps at component makers threaten output and delay EV expansion

items.title: Telecom tower companies eye 5–6% tenancy growth on network push
items.summary: Telecom tower companies anticipate strong tenancy growth of five to six percent in the coming two fiscal years. This surge is fueled by telecom operators expanding their networks. This revival will boost tenancy ratios at existing sites. Tower companies will invest in new towers and upgrades. This investment will improve operational efficiency and profitability for the sector.

items.title: Digital systems secure, says Axis Bank amid AI platform-driven vulnerability claims on cybersecurity
items.summary: Axis Bank assures its digital systems are secure. The bank states cyber fraud largely occurs when customers are tricked by external communications, not due to weaknesses in its own cybersecurity. Officials highlighted that advanced AI models like Mythos can accelerate vulnerability discovery. Axis Bank is enhancing its response to identified weaknesses and deploying new security tools to protect customers.

items.title: US stocks: US market ticks up ahead of first Fed policy decision under Warsh
items.summary: U.S. stock indexes opened higher on Wednesday, with chip stocks rebounding ahead of the Federal Reserve's first interest rate decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite all saw gains in early trading.

items.title: India-EU free trade deal to be inked by year-end: Ursula von der Leyen

items.title: Base Metals Weekly Report: Motilal Oswal
items.summary: According to Motilal Oswal, Base metals traded choppy last week, with prices hitting multi month highs at the start of the week and followed by some profit taking on back of volatility in the dollar and mixed data signals from China and US.

items.title: Flipkart records highest-ever single day sale, grosses $200mn
items.summary: This is the biggest ever single day sale for Bangalore-based Flipkart, since the time the company started in 2007.

items.title: Subramanian Swamy seeks ban on import of cement from Pakistan
items.summary: "Allowing imports of cement from Pakistan, therefore, carried with it the additional risk in that it provides an effective cover for smuggling of contraband goods and harmful weapons and ammunition concealed in cement bags which comes in rakes and trucks, in the hands of disruptionist elements," he said.

items.title: Moneycontrol Exclusive: Govt plans overhaul of tax administration for smooth GST rollout
items.summary: The government has readied plans for a major change in India’s tax administration structure, including overhauling the Central Board of Excise and Customs (CBEC) to ensure a glitch-free roll-out of a nation-wide goods and services tax (GST).

items.title: Sensex, Nifty wobbly; Hind Zinc, SBI, Force Motors most active
items.summary: Asian Paints, SBI, Tata Motors, HUL and Maruti Suzuki are top gainers while ONGC, Axis Bank, MM, Bajaj Auto and Hero MotoCorp are major losers in the Sensex.

items.title: Uber  redBus partner with ixigo for train travellers
items.summary: ixigo, India’s leading mobile travel marketplace has announced partnerships with Uber and redBus to enable instant bookings for cabs and buses natively within ixigo trains app.

items.title: Workload in IT to come down due to AI; jobs to reduce: Genpact
items.summary: But along with that the employment growth rates have started to dip, NV 'Tiger' Tyagarajan says. The percentage addition of employees in India will not be same as past. And due to advancements, a workforce with higher skill sets is required for the IT industry, he says.

items.title: Pixel launch: Is Google Assistant's AI an assault on privacy?
items.summary: Sure, artificial intelligence can make life simpler by suggesting you places to eat, tell you weather, or even crack a joke.  The more you use it, the more it learns about your habits and preferences. The protocols pertaining to artificial intelligence, or the lack of it, make our data less secure and more vulnerable.

items.title: Up over 200% in 1 year, what's buzzing with Borosil Glass?
items.summary: Shares of Borosil Glass Works have increased threefold over the past one year as the company has posted strong revenue and EBIDTA growth over the past few quarters.

items.title: Why you should not be always chasing returns
items.summary: Most people focus on returns and chase products that offer the best returns at any point.

items.title: Jhunjhunwala-partnered fund buys stake in TFCI: What's buzzing?
items.summary: In an interview with CNBC-TV18, Satpal Arora, MD of Tourism Finance Corporation of India (TFCI), talked about the state of business and the outlook ahead.

items.title: RBI latest meeting 'diluted' its old policy framework: Nomura
items.summary: On Tuesday, the central bank surprised the market with a 25-basis-point cut to its policy repo rate, or the rate at which it lends to banks, to 6.25 percent.

items.title: Bond bull market may pause but is far from over: Expert
items.summary: The benchmark 10-year government-security yield remained stuck in 8-7.5 percent range through all of 2015 and half of 2016, moving lower to sub-7 percent only when the RBI promised in April to reduce the system's liquidity deficit. The yield may now fall more.

items.title: Scope for meaningful rate cuts going ahead: Neelkanth Mishra
items.summary: Credit Suisse' Neelkanth Mishra expects repo rate to fall to a decade low in the coming quarters. He says that beginning December the market may see a robust and widespread pick-up which may boost indices.

items.title: Tech up to a healthier world
items.summary: Sensors, 3D printed skin, wearables, reconstructed organs, revolutionized clinical trials, personalized medicine and what not – get ready for a new era of healthcare

items.title: Steel stocks rally as govt extends MIP on 66 steel products
items.summary: Stocks like Hindustan Zinc, Hindalco, Jindal Steel, JSW Steel and Tata Steel gained over 1 percent from previous close.

items.title: Endurance Technologies IPO opens: Should you subscribe?
items.summary: The Aurangabad-based company aims to mop up nearly Rs 1,162 crore (at higher end of price band) by diluting 17.5 percent stake through the issue that will close on October 7. It already raised Rs 348.52 crore through anchor investors' portion on Tuesday, the day before issue opening.

items.title: Cautiously optimistic on India; eyeing earnings revival: Sanger
items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
